sections.title: Phrasal verbs
items: **put up** — put something on a wall (eg, a picture)

exercises.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

items.sentence: The weather ...... says there will be strong winds tomorrow.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is weather prediction, so the correct answer is "forecast".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Our school teaches students to ...... bottles and cans.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is reuse materials, so the correct answer is "recycle".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: We must ...... forests for future generations.

explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: Circle the correct word
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentenceBefore: Mars is a ...... in our solar system.
items.originalSentence: Mars is a ...... in our solar system. **planet / satellite**
items.completedSentence: Mars is a ...... in our solar system. planet

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "planet" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "planet" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "satellite" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.satellite: "satellite" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceBefore: The lake was almost ...... after the very cold night.
items.originalSentence: The lake was almost ...... after the very cold night. **local / freezing**
items.completedSentence: The lake was almost ...... after the very cold night. freezing

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "freezing" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "freezing" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "local" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.local: "local" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "extinct" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "extinct" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "wild" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.wild: "wild" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceBefore: Volunteers helped to ...... the injured bird.
items.originalSentence: Volunteers helped to ...... the injured bird. **name / rescue**
items.completedSentence: Volunteers helped to ...... the injured bird. rescue

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "rescue" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "rescue" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "name" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.name: "name" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceBefore: A dolphin is a ...... because it feeds its babies milk.
items.originalSentence: A dolphin is a ...... because it feeds its babies milk. **mammal / reptile**
items.completedSentence: A dolphin is a ...... because it feeds its babies milk. mammal

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "mammal" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "mammal" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "reptile" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.reptile: "reptile" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence.

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceBefore: The stars looked ...... through the telescope.
items.originalSentence: The stars looked ...... through the telescope. **amazing / global**

exercises.title: Phrasal verbs (complete the gaps)
exercises.instructions: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.

items.sentence: Please ...... that box and come here.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is place down, so the correct answer is "put down".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "put down", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: The sky is dark now, but it should ...... later.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is become fine, so the correct answer is "clear up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "clear up", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The fence is there to ...... people from the protected beach.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is block entry, so the correct answer is "keep out".

explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "keep out" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence: They used water to ...... the campfire.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is extinguish, so the correct answer is "put out".

explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "put up" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

exercises.title: Word patterns
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: My little sister is afraid ...... storms.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Afraid of storms.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Underline time expressions first — they usually tell you which tense to use.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "of" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "The campsite was short ...... food after the storm.".

exercises.title: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The park is in a ...... area of the town. (CENTRE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from CENTRE, so the correct answer is "central".

wrongOptions.centre: The root CENTRE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "central".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of CENTRE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: CENTRE → central: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "central" is the word-formation answer from the root CENTRE in this context.

items.sentence: The moon looked almost ...... last night. (CIRCLE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from CIRCLE, so the correct answer is "circular".

wrongOptions.circle: The root CIRCLE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "circular".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of CIRCLE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: CIRCLE → circular: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "circular" is the word-formation answer from the root CIRCLE in this context.

wrongOptions.danger: The root DANGER cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "dangerous".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DANGER that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: DANGER → dangerous: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "dangerous" is the word-formation answer from the root DANGER in this context.
